description_long: This is a cylindrical stone tower with a robust, fortress-like appearance, featuring a wide base and a narrower upper section. The exterior is constructed from light gray stone blocks, giving it a medieval, fortified look. The roof is conical and covered with blue shingles, with a smaller, matching blue-roofed turret protruding from the upper section. Around the midsection, there are several blue-tiled awnings supported by wooden brackets, adding visual interest and architectural detail. The tower has small, slit-like windows distributed around its circumference, typical of defensive structures. There are no visible doors or large openings, and the structure is adorned with subtle stonework and wooden trim.
description: A cylindrical stone tower with blue shingled roofs, wooden trim, and small slit windows, evoking a medieval fortress style.
description_view_0: This view highlights the main cylindrical body, the conical blue roof, and a protruding turret on the upper left side.
description_view_1: The front view shows the full height of the tower, the central blue roof, and the symmetrical arrangement of blue awnings and stonework.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the right side of the tower, with the protruding turret more visible and the blue awnings wrapping around the midsection.
description_view_3: The rear view displays the back of the tower, with the blue roof and awnings, and the absence of the protruding turret.
